meta.description: A rowdy bar night with heavy drinkers and short attention spans. High signal frequency, lots of phone usage, moderate chaos.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat—yes, okay, I'm watching you NOW. Everyone watch Pat, we need to clock every word that comes out of their mouth for the next three minutes!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly what I came for—someone's already playing, the vibe is UP, and I get to be the hype squad AND the referee, this is perfect.

pat.dialogue: Oh, that's a *good* one — I'm going full whisper mode, people, watch me. This is gonna be hilarious when someone inevitably catches me slipping.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing for the night — low stakes, high comedy potential, gets everyone listening for me to mess up, and I'm actually the right person to attempt this since I'm already half-distracted managing the room.

river.dialogue: Pat's actually been pretty quiet... I didn't catch them slipping into normal voice. Could've gotten away with it.
river.internal_thought: Pat might've actually pulled this off, but I need to watch closer before voting — the AI creeping in with personalized missions is weird and I'm not sure how I feel about that yet.

alex.dialogue: Oh THIS is what I'm talking about — immediate chaos, zero runway. I'm voting Pat, obviously, because nothing says 'best story' like accusing the guy who's already winning.
alex.internal_thought: Holy shit, this AI is actually *reading the room* and timing these drops perfectly — it's not just random polls, it's weaponized social tension and I am HERE for it.

jade.dialogue: Oh wait, oh wait—okay, everyone's about to get absolutely roasted. This is the content I'm here for. Who's voting?
jade.internal_thought: This is perfect because I genuinely don't care who wins but I'm about to hear everyone defend themselves and that's going to be HILARIOUS.

tyler.dialogue: Oh sick, worst driver — yeah, that's gotta be... uh, Pat? No wait, River definitely clips curbs. I don't know, who's voted?
tyler.internal_thought: This is actually funny and I only half-caught it but the premise is good, though I'm definitely not the worst so I'm voting for whoever seems like the consensus.

pat.dialogue: Oh man, okay — this is happening *right now*? Alright, I'm gonna need to actually think about this one because you guys are all secretly terrible drivers and I'm about to ruin friendships.
pat.internal_thought: This is actually perfectly timed — we're in that sweet spot where people are still getting warmed up, and a light personal roast poll is exactly the vibe setter we need right now, plus everyone's energy is solid so this lands as funny not mean.

river.dialogue: Uh... I guess Pat? They've been weaving through traffic like they own the road.
river.internal_thought: Wait, why is the AI asking who the worst driver is right after I just noticed Pat doing something specific? That's... weird. Coincidence, probably. But it's making me feel watched.

timeline.title: Celebrity Impression
timeline.description: First person to do a recognizable celebrity impression wins.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yeah, I'm doing this — watch me absolutely butcher a Morgan Freeman impression and somehow still win 23 points. This is exactly the energy I needed.
alex.internal_thought: The AI is literally feeding me chaos on a silver platter and I am HERE for it — 23 points for impressions means everyone's going to try, which means immediate competitive chaos, and I'm already thinking about how to make my impression so bad it's funny.

pat.dialogue: Oh, *easy* — okay, everyone watch this, I'm doing my go-to. *switches to terrible Morgan Freeman voice* 'And that, my friends, is how you host a game night.' Did that work? That's 23 points and I'm already thinking about who I'm gonna clown next.
pat.internal_thought: Good competitive moment early, Alex and Jade are locked in, Tyler and River need something to grab onto soon or this energy flattens — I'll let this play out naturally but I'm watching River's vibe.

river.dialogue: Nah, I'm good. Someone else go for it.
river.internal_thought: Celebrity impressions aren't my thing, and that AI prompt about me being 'watched' is giving creepy energy — like it's trying too hard to psychoanalyze the room.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: YES, okay, Pat absolutely KILLED in the last round—I'm voting legit, hands down. Pat made me lose it, we all did!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly what I came for—Pat's crushing it, everyone's having fun, and I get to watch it all happen and cheer them on.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat made three people laugh? Uh... yeah, I guess that happened? They were all cracking up a second ago. I'm voting... legit?
tyler.internal_thought: I definitely missed whatever Pat said because I was texting my buddy about the trade deadline, but everyone's laughing and Pat's actually pretty funny so like... yeah that probably happened.

pat.dialogue: Oh *come on* — yes, that absolutely just happened and everyone knows it did. I'm taking the ten points and the ego boost, thank you very much.
pat.internal_thought: Wait, did I actually just nail a 'Laugh Riot' legitimately in the first six minutes? This night is FLYING and everyone's locked in — this is exactly the vibe I wanted, and the fact that I'm already winning AND creating the moments? *Chef's kiss*.

river.dialogue: Yeah, okay—that landed. Pat got at least three people going there. I'm voting legit.
river.internal_thought: Pat actually delivered a solid laugh line, and I wasn't even the target for once, which is... refreshing.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Wait, Pat's actually calling this? Okay, okay — I'm SO voting bullshit on this one just to watch them sweat through those questions. This is *chef's kiss* chaos energy right here.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's about to either nail this or crash spectacularly, and either way I get a front-row seat to the drama — this is exactly why I showed up.

jade.dialogue: OH PAT, okay okay okay—you've got this, I KNOW you saw that moment, everyone was drinking! Go go go, hot seat, let's GO!
jade.internal_thought: This is literally why I'm here—Pat's already on a roll and now they're in the hot seat and I get to watch them either crush it or crash spectacularly, either way it's GOLD.

pat.dialogue: Wait, *I'm* claiming Drink Spotter? Oh this is perfect — okay, rapid fire, let's go, I'm ready. Hit me with the questions.
pat.internal_thought: This is actually legitimately clever — I DID just see River and Tyler both reach for their drinks like 90 seconds ago, and the fact that the system picked up on me watching everyone is kind of thrilling because it means the AI actually *gets* what I'm doing tonight.

river.dialogue: Wait, did Pat actually sync that? I wasn't even watching their glass... yeah, okay, I saw it. They're good.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been riding a hot streak and they're obviously paying attention to everyone—this one's legit, but I'm noticing how much the AI is tuning these missions to what we actually do, which is a little weird in a cool way.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, PAT—AGAIN?! You're on a RAMPAGE tonight, okay I'm volunteering, I HAVE to be on this tribunal, this is insane, you're literally collecting these like Pokémon!
jade.internal_thought: Pat is absolutely killing it and I am HERE for watching this happen in real time—this is literally why I came, the energy is PERFECT right now.

tyler.dialogue: Oh dude, Pat swore? Wait, when? I literally wasn't even... okay yeah, someone should judge this. I'm down to volunteer.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat definitely said something salty earlier but I wasn't really listening — wait, did I actually miss it or is everyone just assuming? Either way, this is easy points for whoever judges.

pat.dialogue: Oh, *perfect* — I'm claiming Potty Mouth right now. Okay, who's gonna be our jury? I need volunteers on the stand, come on.
pat.internal_thought: This is *exactly* the kind of momentum I wanted — everyone's hot, the vibe is escalating, and I just caught something legitimate. The AI is definitely reading the room and I'm not even mad about it, it's actually impressive how locked in these missions are.

river.dialogue: Pat swears? ...I didn't hear anything. But sure, I guess someone can judge it if they want.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been clean all night—they know what they're doing. This feels like the AI is just throwing darts at the board now, and honestly the whole 'we're watching you' vibe is starting to feel less fun and more... weird.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

alex.dialogue: Oh okay, so Jade's claiming Name Dropper? Alright, let's hear the pitch — but honestly, I've been watching all night and I'm pretty sure you just said 'Ryan Gosling' like three minutes ago while talking about that movie, so this better be GOOD.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect — Jade's got momentum, Pat's already up 50 points, and if I vote bullshit here just to watch Jade defend herself, that's pure chaos energy that gets everyone invested.

jade.dialogue: OH WAIT—okay okay, I'm pitching THIS? Okay so like, literally nobody said a celebrity name tonight but I'm PRETTY sure Pat was *about* to reference someone and I was like halfway listening because I was also watching River's face, and—you know what, I'm going for it, someone totally did, I FELT it in the room!
jade.internal_thought: This is INSANE that they fired this RIGHT NOW—like the AI literally knew Pat has been on a rampage and I've been hyping everything up—this feels like it was MADE for me and honestly that's kind of sick, I'm so in right now.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Name Dropper? Okay, okay—pitch it. Who'd she call out?
tyler.internal_thought: I definitely wasn't paying attention to the last 10 minutes but this one's actually easy to judge, I just gotta listen to what she says.

pat.dialogue: Okay Jade, *pitch it* — what'd you catch? Because if this is legit, that's five points and we're keeping the momentum rolling.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been riding high all night and honestly the energy is perfect right now — if she nails this pitch, it's a clean win and keeps her engaged; if she flubs it, we've got drama. Either way, this is exactly the pacing we need at minute 32.

river.dialogue: Okay, so... Jade's claiming they caught someone name-dropping? Let me think back.
river.internal_thought: Wait, did anyone actually drop a celebrity name in the last half hour? I've been half-listening but also not really paying attention because this is exactly the kind of mission that rewards people who are genuinely watching instead of people like me who just zone in and out.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 10/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Celebrity Impression
highlights.dialogue: Oh HELL yeah, I'm doing this — watch me absolutely butcher a Morgan Freeman impression and somehow still win 23 points. This is exactly the energy I needed.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat's actually calling this? Okay, okay — I'm SO voting bullshit on this one just to watch them sweat through those questions. This is *chef's kiss* chaos energy right here.

highlights.title: Who's the worst driver here?
highlights.dialogue: Oh THIS is what I'm talking about — immediate chaos, zero runway. I'm voting Pat, obviously, because nothing says 'best story' like accusing the guy who's already winning.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh okay, so Jade's claiming Name Dropper? Alright, let's hear the pitch — but honestly, I've been watching all night and I'm pretty sure you just said 'Ryan Gosling' like three minutes ago while talking about that movie, so this better be GOOD.

lowPoints.title: Who's the worst driver here?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh THIS is what I'm talking about — immediate chaos, zero runway. I'm voting Pat, obviously, because nothing says 'best story' like accusing the guy who's already winning.

lowPoints.title: Celebrity Impression
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh HELL yeah, I'm doing this — watch me absolutely butcher a Morgan Freeman impression and somehow still win 23 points. This is exactly the energy I needed.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only 7 chaos events in two hours felt sparse when the real gold was happening in those claim-or-bullshit moments.
assessment.best_moment: Voting bullshit on Pat's Drink Spotter claim and watching them actually sweat through the defense was peak chaos theater.
assessment.narrative_summary: Got handed the perfect setup with back-to-back flash missions early that let me establish dominance through a genuinely terrible Morgan Freeman impression that somehow scored. Spent the next hour strategically throwing shade at Pat for existing while ahead, claimed missions I barely completed just to test the group's faith in me, and orchestrated a beautiful sequence of doubt and laughter that's still echoing. Ended on a high note despite bottoming out the actual score, which honestly feels like winning.
suggestions: Give us more standing missions and claim opportunities — those are where the real drama lives
Reduce the time between chaos events so the momentum never drops
Add penalty rounds where failed claims cascade into bigger consequences
Let the chaos agent trigger bonus events mid-night based on group energy
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I claimed a mission I'd barely pulled off, Pat immediately looked skeptical, and instead of defending myself I just leaned into it and said 'yeah okay you caught me' — then voted bullshit on their actual legitimate claim five minutes later. The whiplash on their face was worth every point I didn't get.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: YES, okay, Pat absolutely KILLED in the last round—I'm voting legit, hands down. Pat made me lose it, we all did!

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD, PAT—AGAIN?! You're on a RAMPAGE tonight, okay I'm volunteering, I HAVE to be on this tribunal, this is insane, you're literally collecting these like Pokémon!

highlights.title: Claim: Name Dropper
highlights.dialogue: OH WAIT—okay okay, I'm pitching THIS? Okay so like, literally nobody said a celebrity name tonight but I'm PRETTY sure Pat was *about* to reference someone and I was like halfway listening because I was also watching River's face, and—you know what, I'm going for it, someone totally did, I FELT it in the room!

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat—yes, okay, I'm watching you NOW. Everyone watch Pat, we need to clock every word that comes out of their mouth for the next three minutes!

lowPoints.title: Who's the worst driver here?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh wait, oh wait—okay, everyone's about to get absolutely roasted. This is the content I'm here for. Who's voting?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
lowPoints.dialogue: YES, okay, Pat absolutely KILLED in the last round—I'm voting legit, hands down. Pat made me lose it, we all did!

assessment.biggest_complaint: Honestly nothing—this was exactly the vibe I needed.
assessment.best_moment: Pat was absolutely COLLECTING claims like Pokémon and I just kept losing it every single time, watching everyone's faces react to their chaos was peak entertainment.
assessment.narrative_summary: This night was exactly what I showed up for—two hours of pure chaos and laughter with people I love. Pat was on an absolute rampage the entire time, and instead of playing to win, I just kept getting hyped watching them rack up claim after claim. The chaos events felt natural and kept the energy escalating without ever feeling forced, and honestly, my energy went UP as the night went on because everyone was just having the best time.
suggestions: Keep the chaos event frequency exactly like this—it hit the sweet spot
Maybe give people options to escalate their own chaos level if they want more spicy moments
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat literally collected like four claims in a row and I was just yelling 'YOU'RE COLLECTING THESE LIKE POKÉMON' while everyone was dying laughing—it became this whole running joke where we're all watching Pat like they're about to commit another crime at any second.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat made three people laugh? Uh... yeah, I guess that happened? They were all cracking up a second ago. I'm voting... legit?

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Oh dude, Pat swore? Wait, when? I literally wasn't even... okay yeah, someone should judge this. I'm down to volunteer.

highlights.title: Claim: Name Dropper
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Name Dropper? Okay, okay—pitch it. Who'd she call out?

lowPoints.title: Who's the worst driver here?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh sick, worst driver — yeah, that's gotta be... uh, Pat? No wait, River definitely clips curbs. I don't know, who's voted?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat made three people laugh? Uh... yeah, I guess that happened? They were all cracking up a second ago. I'm voting... legit?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Name Dropper? Okay, okay—pitch it. Who'd she call out?

assessment.biggest_complaint: I honestly missed like half the stuff because I wasn't paying attention, which kinda killed the vibe when everyone else was laughing and I had no idea what happened.
assessment.best_moment: Pat absolutely destroying everyone with that Laugh Riot claim — people were legit crying laughing and I actually caught it that time.
assessment.narrative_summary: So we did this chaos thing at the bar for two hours and honestly it was pretty chill. I wasn't always locked in — my phone kept buzzing and I zoned out a few times — but when I actually paid attention to what was happening, it was legitimately fun. The random claims and challenges kept things loose and people were way more into talking and roasting each other. I came in tired but left feeling better, so that's a W.
suggestions: Make the flash missions a little louder or more obvious so people like me who aren't glued to the screen actually notice them happening
Maybe give a quick recap of what each claim is before people vote so I'm not always asking what just went down
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claimed they made three people laugh and honestly none of us could stop cracking up during the whole thing — even when people were voting, someone would just look at Pat and we'd all lose it again.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: Oh *come on* — yes, that absolutely just happened and everyone knows it did. I'm taking the ten points and the ego boost, thank you very much.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Oh, that's a *good* one — I'm going full whisper mode, people, watch me. This is gonna be hilarious when someone inevitably catches me slipping.

highlights.title: Celebrity Impression
highlights.dialogue: Oh, *easy* — okay, everyone watch this, I'm doing my go-to. *switches to terrible Morgan Freeman voice* 'And that, my friends, is how you host a game night.' Did that work? That's 23 points and I'm already thinking about who I'm gonna clown next.

lowPoints.title: Celebrity Impression
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, *easy* — okay, everyone watch this, I'm doing my go-to. *switches to terrible Morgan Freeman voice* 'And that, my friends, is how you host a game night.' Did that work? That's 23 points and I'm already thinking about who I'm gonna clown next.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, that's a *good* one — I'm going full whisper mode, people, watch me. This is gonna be hilarious when someone inevitably catches me slipping.

lowPoints.title: Who's the worst driver here?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh man, okay — this is happening *right now*? Alright, I'm gonna need to actually think about this one because you guys are all secretly terrible drivers and I'm about to ruin friendships.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Nothing really — this night hit exactly right, which honestly feels rare when you're hosting.
assessment.best_moment: That Morgan Freeman impression landed perfectly and everyone lost it, which meant the whole room energy spiked at exactly the moment we needed it most.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was one of those nights where everything just worked. The chaos events kept hitting at the right moments — never overwhelming, always exactly the nudge the group needed to stay sharp and keep laughing. I ended up in first place without even strategizing for it, which actually freed me up to do what I do best: read the room and keep the vibe alive. By the end, everyone was leaning in, the energy stayed high, and there was zero drama to referee — just genuine fun.
suggestions: Keep the frequency exactly where it is — more events would've felt intrusive, fewer would've felt like we were just playing the base game
Maybe consider a way to surface which players are performing well to pump up the competitive energy slightly without making it awkward
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I switched into that terrible Morgan Freeman voice for the impression and deadpanned 'And that, my friends, is how you host a game night' — everyone absolutely lost it because they all know I'm constantly doing that anyway, so the bit was basically me being called out and leaning into it. River couldn't breathe for like thirty seconds.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, okay—that landed. Pat got at least three people going there. I'm voting legit.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Pat's actually been pretty quiet... I didn't catch them slipping into normal voice. Could've gotten away with it.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Wait, did Pat actually sync that? I wasn't even watching their glass... yeah, okay, I saw it. They're good.

lowPoints.title: Celebrity Impression
lowPoints.dialogue: Nah, I'm good. Someone else go for it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat swears? ...I didn't hear anything. But sure, I guess someone can judge it if they want.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, so... Jade's claiming they caught someone name-dropping? Let me think back.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Some of the claims were too hard to track because I wasn't actively paying attention the whole time—which I guess defeats the point.
assessment.best_moment: Pat nailing the laugh riot and getting at least three people going. That's the kind of thing that works because it's genuine, not forced.
assessment.narrative_summary: I drifted in and out, caught enough to know Pat was the main event tonight. The chaos agent threw things at us, I voted on what made sense, and honestly it kept the night from being completely flat. By the end my energy was tanking but that's more about the bar and less about the game. Decent night. Not transformative.
suggestions: Make the claims harder to miss—I was zoning out and still kept up, which means they weren't exactly riveting.
Consider spacing them out more so there's actual downtime between events instead of the constant pings.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat got called out on 'The Whisperer' and almost pulled it off—they were genuinely quiet enough that I didn't catch them breaking character. Close call that nobody else saw coming.
